title: Rankine Earth Pressure Theory
content: Rankine's theory provides active (Ka) and passive (Kp) lateral earth pressure coefficients: Ka=(1-sinφ)/(1+sinφ), Kp=1/Ka, where φ is the internal friction angle. Active pressure at depth z: σa=Ka·γ·z−2c·√Ka, where γ is soil unit weight and c is cohesion. Used to design retaining walls for overturning and sliding stability.
